*�����������������k# me - this DAT
# par - the Par object that has changed
# val - the current value
# prev - the previous value
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the Parameter Execute DAT.

def onValueChange(par, prev):
	# use par.eval() to get cuurrent value
	match par.name:
		case 'Videocodec':
			ext.RecordExt.videoCodec()
		case _:
			return

# Called at end of frame with complete list of individual parameter changes.
# The changes are a list of named tuples, where each tuple is (Par, previous value)
def onValuesChanged(changes):
	for c in changes:
		# use par.eval() to get current value
		par = c.par
		prev = c.prev
	return

def onPulse(par):
	match par.name:
		case 'Recordstart':
			ext.RecordExt.recordStart()
		case 'Recordstop':
			ext.RecordExt.recordStop()
		case 'Matchrange':
			ext.RecordExt.matchRange()
		case 'Matchresolution':
			ext.RecordExt.matchResolution()
		case 'Reload':
			ext.RecordExt.findMovieTop()
			ext.RecordExt.loadMeta()
		case 'Matchmedia':
			ext.RecordExt.matchToMedia()
		case 'Matchtimelinetorecord':
			ext.RecordExt.matchTimelineToRecord()
		case 'Matchrecordtotimeline':
			ext.RecordExt.matchRecordToTimeline()
		case _:
			#default case
			return

def onExpressionChange(par, val, prev):
	return

def onExportChange(par, val, prev):
	return

def onEnableChange(par, val, prev):
	return

def onModeChange(par, val, prev):
	return

2
*�����������������k"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner components with python. An
extension is accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from any operator
within the extended component. If the extension is promoted via its
Promote Extension parameter, all its attributes with capitalized names
can be accessed externally, e.g. op('yourComp').PromotedFunction().

Help: search "Extensions" in wiki
"""

from TDStoreTools import StorageManager
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

class RecordExt:
	"""
	RecordExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.recordTop = self.ownerComp.op('moviefileout1')

		# properties
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'movieInTop', value=None, dependable=True,
						   readOnly=False)

		self.infoChop = self.ownerComp.op('info1')
		self.infoDat = self.ownerComp.op('info2')
		self.recordTop = self.ownerComp.op('moviefileout1')
		self.timerChop = self.ownerComp.op('timer1')

		self.previousRangeLimit = self.ownerComp.time.par.rangelimit.eval()
		self.previousRealTime = project.realTime
		self.previousFrame = 1
		self.previousPlayState = None

		self.movieInTop = None

		self.recordStop()

	def videoCodec(self, *args):
		self.refreshMenus()

	def refreshMenus(self):
		self.ownerComp.cook(force=True)

	def findMovieTop(self):
		stepOp = self.ownerComp
		while stepOp.inputs:
			stepOp = stepOp.inputs[0]

		if isinstance(stepOp, moviefileinTOP):
			self.movieInTop = stepOp
			return stepOp
		else:
			ui.status = 'Recorder Custom COMP Info Error: Upstream operator is not a moviefileinTOP.'
			self.movieInTop = None
			return None

	def loadMeta(self):
		"""
		Loads meta information from current movie in top
		"""
		if self.movieInTop:
			self.ownerComp.par.Framerange1 = 1
			self.ownerComp.par.Framerange2 = self.movieInTop.numImages
			self.ownerComp.par.Rate = self.movieInTop.rate

	def recordStart(self):
		"""
		Sets up system for recording
		"""
		self.ownerComp.par.Recordstart.enable = False
		self.ownerComp.par.Recordstop.enable = True
		self.ownerComp.op('timer1').par.active = 'running'
		self.ownerComp.op('timer1').par.initialize.pulse()
		self.ownerComp.op('timer1').par.start.pulse()
		self.recordTop.par.n = self.recordTop.par.n.eval() + 1  
		self.recordTop.par.record = True
		self.previousRangeLimit = self.ownerComp.time.par.rangelimit.eval()
		if self.ownerComp.par.Recordrange.eval():
			self.ownerComp.time.par.rangelimit = 'hold'
		self.previousFrame = self.ownerComp.time.frame
		self.ownerComp.time.frame = 1
		self.previousPlayState = self.ownerComp.time.play
		self.ownerComp.time.play = False
		me.time.play = True
		#run('me.time.play = True', delayFrames=1, fromOP=self.ownerComp, delayRef=self.ownerComp)
		if self.ownerComp.par.Cookeveryframe.eval():
			self.previousRealTime = project.realTime
			project.realTime = False

	def recordStop(self):
		"""
		Sets parameters for no recording
		"""
		self.ownerComp.par.Recordstart.enable = True
		self.ownerComp.par.Recordstop.enable = False

		#Removed turn off record because it happens automatically now
		self.recordTop.par.record = False

		self.ownerComp.time.par.rangelimit = self.previousRangeLimit
		self.ownerComp.op('timer1').par.active = 'never'
		project.realTime = self.previousRealTime
		self.ownerComp.time.play = self.previousPlayState
		self.ownerComp.time.frame = self.previousFrame

	def matchRange(self):
		"""
		Gets the range from movie
		"""
		movieTop = self.findMovieTop()
		if movieTop:
			self.ownerComp.time.start = 1
			self.ownerComp.time.end = movieTop.numImages
			self.ownerComp.time.rangeStart = 1
			self.ownerComp.time.rangeEnd = movieTop.numImages
			self.ownerComp.time.rate = movieTop.rate

	def matchRecordToTimeline(self):
		self.ownerComp.par.Framerange1 = self.ownerComp.time.start
		self.ownerComp.par.Framerange2 = self.ownerComp.time.end
		self.ownerComp.par.Rate = self.ownerComp.time.rate

	def matchTimelineToRecord(self):
		range1 = self.ownerComp.par.Framerange1.eval()
		range2 = self.ownerComp.par.Framerange2.eval()
		if range2 > self.ownerComp.time.end:
			self.ownerComp.time.end = range2
			self.ownerComp.time.rangeEnd = range2
		self.ownerComp.time.rangeStart  = range1
		self.ownerComp.time.rangeEnd = range2
		self.ownerComp.time.rate = self.ownerComp.par.Rate 

	def matchToMedia(self):
		moviefilein = self.findNode(self.ownerComp, 'moviefilein')
		if moviefilein:
			infos = self.getInfos(moviefilein)
			if infos:
				self.ownerComp.time.start = 1
				self.ownerComp.time.rate = infos['rate']
				self.ownerComp.time.end = infos['numImages']
				self.ownerComp.time.rangeStart = 1
				self.ownerComp.time.rangeEnd = infos['numImages']

	def getInfos(self, moviefilein):
		"""
		Gets the length in frames of a moviefilein 
		"""
		if moviefilein.isOpen:
			numImages = moviefilein.numImages
			numSeconds = moviefilein.numSeconds
			rate = moviefilein.rate
			infos = {'rate': rate, 'numSeconds':numSeconds, 'numImages': numImages}
			return infos
		else:
			return None

	def findNode(self, node, type):
		while node:  # Will terminate if node has no inputs i.e., when node = None
			if node.type == 'moviefilein':
				return node
			else:
				if node.inputs:
					node = node.inputs[0]  # Move to the input of the current node
				else: 
					ui.status = "No valid moviefilein TOP is found at input, so can't set length"
					return None

	@property
	def filename(self):
		folder = self.ownerComp.par.Folder.eval() 
		folder = folder + '/' if folder else ''
		names = [getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Filename'+str(i+1)).eval() for i in range(7)]
		while('' in names):
			names.remove('')
		return folder + '_'.join(names) + f'_{str(self.recordTop.par.n.eval()).zfill(4)}.mov'

def onOnToOff(channel, sampleIndex, val, prev):

def onValueChange(channel, sampleIndex, val, prev):
	debug(channel.name, val)
	return
